title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
description:
Kth Smallest Element in a BST

LeetCode 230. Kth Smallest Element in a BST

Given the root of a binary search tree and an integer k, return the kth smallest value (1-indexed) of all the values of the nodes in the tree.

Examples:

  • Input: root = [3,1,4,null,2], k = 1
      3
 / \
1   4
 \
  2
    Output: 1
  • Input: root = [5,3,6,2,4,null,null,1], k = 3
          5
     / \
    3   6
   / \
  2   4
 /
1
    Output: 3

Constraints:

  • The number of nodes in the tree is n
  • 1 <= k <= n <= 104
  • 0 <= Node.val <= 104
template:
# Definition for a binary tree node.
# class TreeNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.left = left
#         self.right = right

def kthSmallest(root, k):
solution_text:
Kth Smallest Element in a BST

Algorithm:

  1. In-order traversal of a BST visits nodes in ascending order
  2. Use an iterative stack-based in-order traversal
  3. Decrement a counter each time you process a node; when it reaches 0, return that node's value

Time Complexity: O(h + k) — traverses at most h + k nodes, where h is tree height

Space Complexity: O(h) — stack holds one path from root to current node

Key Insight: BST in-order traversal yields sorted values for free — the kth node visited is the kth smallest. The iterative approach lets you stop early as soon as you've counted k nodes.
